cards.definition:
enucleation= the entire eye
eVISceration= leaves a VISible sclera
Prosthesis fitting begins ~8 weeks post surgery
Need for socket stimulation in infancy - stimulates bony orbit to grow

done prior to making a prosthetic, under anesthesia and casted in stone or wax
Progresses to every couple months after ~12mos of age until growth slows (~age 2 years)

horizontal palpebral fissure may be smaller than the other eye, prescribe polycarb for monocular, high mag to make eye look bigger

Surgery 
 Lateral canthotomy- fast, but can cause scarring and entropion causing further fitting concerns 
 Bony orbit reconstruction (risky, for older patients) + lateral canthotomy 

Progressive spring expander 
 Constant pressure on medial and lateral canthus for 2-3+ weeks

First prosthetic/scleral shell fit between 1-2 years of age
 First f/u after 2 months, then q4-6mos until age 5 years, then q6-9mos until age 10.

In childhood, typically changed 3-4 times by age 10 years
 Face shape changes, normal growth, and sometimes subtle iris color changes

After age 10, f/u with ocularist occur annually
In adulthood, prosthesis is changed every 8-10 years

mechanical rubbing or deposits/substances on surface
Patients with asthma, hay fever, or other allergies more likely to develop (CL and artificial eye pts)
Unlike CL, prostheses and shells are NOT removed during treatment because socket can shrink
Refer to oculoplastic surgeon

hard CL enzymatic solution 1x a month
*Alcohol, solvents, or other chemicals can damage a prosthetic in < 60 seconds
if acrylic do not immerse
Safe disinfectants if soaked < 10 min
store in sterile saline

daily cleaning with baby shampoo
sleep with prosthetic 
remove the acrylic part of the prosthetic ~1x/month and wash well with soap
Polishing is necessary every 6-12 months

cards.term: Department of Children and Families (DCF) definition of child abuse
cards.definition:
Child Protective Services (CPS)= In MA, CPS is called Department of Child and Family Services (DCF)

non-accidental commission of any act by a caregiver which causes physical or emotional abuse, or failure to act

other definitions foe abuse of adults and disabled:
Adult Protective Services
Disabled Persons Protection Commission (DPPC)

report is made when an official capacity, suspects or has reasons to believe that a child has been abused or neglected

51A report= Report of suspected or evidence of any type of Child Abuse or Neglect

Reasonable Cause= A suspicion that a child may be abused, neglected, exploited

• Protects reporter from civil or criminal actions
• "Good faith" = assumption that the reporter had reason to believe that the child in question was being abused
if you have reasonable cause you have immunity

Consequences of Not Filing:
•Up to a $5000 fine
•Prison up to 2.5 years
•Lose professional license

Death, fracture of a bone, subdural hematoma, burns etc
non trivial injury depending on age, number & location
in MA illegal to hit a kid with anything but open fist 
younger children are the most vulnerable to death

Abnormal bruising:
Ears, face, hands and feet Abdomen, back, buttocks, genitals, upper thighs
Pattern Bruising, oddly shaped, lesions, linear

Burns: Any burn is of concern, Story doesn't match w/ injury, round mark- cigarettes

Failure by a caretaker (deliberately neglectful / inability) to provide child w/ minimally adequate food, clothing, shelter, medical care

Educational: Includes not enrolling child in school
Emotional: withholding of affection or attention
Medical: delay or denial of dental or health care

Any non-accidental act by a caregiver upon a child that constitutes a sexual offence under the laws (of MA)

sexual exploitation= ny person under age 18 who has been subjected to victim of sex trafficking in exchange for food or clothing

an impairment to the psychological capacity of a
child

Once you become suspicious of abuse or neglect 
1. Immediately consult with supervisor
2. Immediately make a verbal report to DCF, 15A legally 24hrs
3. File on the Virtual Gateway on mass.gov (very easy to do)

• Names, DOB, gender of all children in home 
• Name, address & telephone of parents
• Language spoken at home
• Professional Data on the reporter (you)
• Description of nature & extent of injury
• How did you find out

1. Call DCF office nearest to where child lives: report will be screened in/out w/in 1 day (also file on virtual)
2. If report is "screened in", DCF has 15 business days to investigate
3. During these 15 days, DCF can talk to anyone; after the 15 days, they need a written consent
4. Emergency: initiated within 2 hours; completed w/in 3 days

Substantiated: enough evidence of concern and will be investigated 
Unsubstantiated: not enough evidence, will not be investigated 
Indicated: should be investigated but family moved or can't be found 
Closed: with no finding Alternative response: voluntary acceptance of CPS, child not hurt 
Open: family will receive assessment & services from DCF

can start 8-11, study shows improved quality of life (no effect on self worth), children cry due to restraint not cl
have steeper and smaller corneas
good candidate has motivation
daily disposable recommended 
f/u 1 week, 3 months, 6 months

glasses:
• Induced base in for myopes 
• Induced base out for hyperopes

contact lenses:
• less BI in myopes, increased convergence demand, exo can decompensate 
• less BO in hyperopes, decreased convergence demand, can improve eso

repeat testing in CL

remove congenital cataracts 4-6weeks of life
CLs usually fit 1-3 weeks after surgery or IOL implantation
if no contact lens or removal by 6mnths, deprivation amblyopia will result

spectacles: non invasive, but poor optics, if unilateral aphakia CL needed for aniseikonia and patching to prevent amblyopia 
IOL: don't have to deal with CL hygiene, invasive

unilateral congenital cataract
either primary IOL implantation or CL correction after surgery

no significant difference in vision
88% developed strabismus in both groups
no effect on glaucoma
more adverse events in IOL group

conclusion: caution in IOL if under 1 year bc
⚪Rate of ocular growth in first year
⚪Potential higher risk of side effects

● Most common CL used for pediatric aphakia
● 100% silicone - hydrophobic

durable, DK 340, extended wear 30 days, can use FL
high lipid deposit, expensive, not many powers available 

silicone and silicone hydrogel less expensive, more comfortable, less durable, no FL, custom made 
RGP used to mask cornea irregularity 

Powers:
children: +20.00 - +32.00 in 3D steps
adults: +11.50 - +20.00 in 0.5D steps

⚪Ideal fit: minimal apical clearance, minimal intermediate bearing; movement 1-2 mm

⚪Too steep if no movement or no fluorescein exchange

⚪Too flat if significant edge lift and excessive movement

● Birth-6 mos: BC 7.5, +29.00
● Over 28 mos: BC 7.9, +18.00
by 5 years adult like

birth to age 1, over-correct by ~+2.00 - +3.00
Once walking, over-correct by ~1.00-+2.00
>2, Polycarbonate bifocal glasses with near add of ~+2.50-+3.00 and any uncorrected astigmatic correction to wear over CLs

cards.term:
Which of the following are contact lenses FDA approved for myopia control? 

A. MiSight
B. Biofinity Toric Multifocal
C. Essilor Stellest
D. Acuvue Abiliti
E. NaturalVue Multifocal
cards.definition: A

cards.term:
Which is TRUE when prescribing soft multifocal contact lenses for myopia control? 

A. Prescribe the lowest add power available 
B. Prescribe center-near CL
C. Prescribe extended wear to maximize wear time
D. Toric parameters are available
cards.definition:
D
want to use higher add, +2.50
center distance, more plus in periphery

nystagmus= CL move with eyes, improves vision
anisometropia= if more than 2-3D of anisometropia cl can correct aniseikonia, good for myopes bc less minification in CL
amblyopia= occlusion therapy, however risk of something happening to good eye 

Prosthetic Tinted Lenses, craniofacial syndromea, irregular corneas

cards.term:
You are fitting a 5 month-old infant with unilateral aphakia OD with a Silsoft. Cycloplegic retinoscopy is OD +20.00 OS +1.00. Corneal diameter is 9.0 mm OD and 10.0 mm OS. Keratometry readings are unavailable. (Note: +20.00 vertexed is +26.25.) What base curve, diameter and power would be the best initial trial lens? 

A. 7.9/11.3/+32.00
B. 7.5/12.5/+26.00
C. 7.5/11.3/+26.00
D. 7.5/11.3/+29.00
cards.definition:
D
+20 vertex to +26, smaller diameter 11.3, smaller base curve, over correct by 3.00 so +29
